chunk_summary: This section outlines prophylactic iron and folic acid (IFA) schedules under Anemia Mukt Bharat, targeting different age groups and delivery platforms, emphasizing lifecycle anemia prevention and integration with deworming and dietary counseling.
learning_objectives: Understand the prophylactic IFA schedules for different age groups under Anemia Mukt Bharat.
Identify delivery platforms for IFA supplementation, including schools, anganwadi centers, and ASHA outreach.
Recognize the importance of aligning prescriptions with state supply chains and legacy programs.
Learn the role of deworming, dietary counseling, and follow-up hemoglobin checks in anemia prevention.
key_facts: Children 6–59 months: Biweekly IFA syrup (1 mL = 20 mg elemental iron + 100 µg folic acid).
School-age children (5–10 years): Weekly IFA tablet (45 mg elemental iron + 400 µg folic acid).
Adolescents (10–19 years): Weekly IFA tablet (60 mg elemental iron + 500 µg folic acid).
Pregnant and lactating women: Daily IFA prophylaxis to prevent maternal anemia and improve infant iron stores.
Older programs like WIFS used different IFA tablet strengths (e.g., 100 mg elemental iron + 500 µg folic acid).
Prophylaxis should be paired with deworming, dietary counseling, and follow-up hemoglobin checks.
reference_quote: AMB operationalizes anemia prevention as a lifecycle strategy delivered through both health and education platforms.
document_key_topics: Prophylactic IFA schedules for different age groups
Integration of deworming and dietary counseling
Challenges with legacy programs and supply chains
